assumptions: The developer already can sign in with an X account or can create one outside the Developer Console flow.
No X developer profile, app, Bearer Token, or API credits exist yet.
The measured goal is pay-per-use X API access rather than Enterprise.

developer_goal: Obtain self-service X API access and complete the official first read-only API v2 request.

first_success.outcome: The developer uses the app Bearer Token to look up a user through X API v2 and receives the user object.
first_success.observable_signal: The cURL user-lookup response includes a JSON data object with id, name, and username.

route_selection.surface: X Developer Console pay-per-use Getting Access plus Make Your First Request
route_selection.rule: Prefer pay-per-use over Enterprise, and the documented fastest first-request test (cURL) over SDK production paths.
route_selection.selected: Pay-per-use Getting Access with cURL user lookup

candidates.name: Pay-per-use Getting Access + Make Your First Request (cURL)
candidates.reason: Overview presents pay-per-use as the no-commitment access level; Getting Access defines account/app/credentials; Make Your First Request calls cURL the fastest test.

candidates.name: X API Enterprise
candidates.reason: Enterprise is positioned for high-volume endpoints, dedicated account management, and custom rate limits.

candidates.name: OAuth user-context first request
candidates.reason: The official first-request guide demonstrates app-only Bearer Token user lookup rather than user-context OAuth.

candidates.name: Official Python or TypeScript SDK first request
candidates.reason: SDKs are recommended for production, while the first-request guide names cURL as the fastest test.

required_path.action: Sign in to console.x.com with an X account to begin developer onboarding.
required_path.observable_result: The Developer Console onboarding flow is available.

required_path.action: Review and accept the Developer Agreement and Policy.
required_path.observable_result: Legal acceptance is recorded for the developer account.

required_path.action: Complete the developer profile with basic information about API use.

required_fields.label: Developer profile / API-use information
required_fields.notes: Getting Access requires basic information but does not publish the exact current form fields or review states.

required_path.observable_result: The developer profile is complete enough to continue to app creation.

required_path.action: Create a new app with name, description, and use case, then save the generated credentials including the Bearer Token.

required_fields.label: Use case
required_fields.notes: Docs require a use case without enumerating the control type or allowed values.

required_path.observable_result: API keys, tokens, and a Bearer Token are generated for the app.

required_path.action: Purchase X API credits upfront in the Developer Console so pay-per-use requests can be charged.

required_fields.label: Credit purchase / payment method
required_fields.notes: Pricing requires purchasing credits upfront; Getting Access omits the payment UI sequence.

required_path.observable_result: The account has credits available for API usage.

required_path.action: Send the documented user-lookup GET request with the Bearer Token Authorization header.
required_path.observable_result: The API returns JSON for the requested user.

required_path.action: Confirm the response data field contains the looked-up user object.
required_path.observable_result: The first-request success criteria are met.

prerequisites.description: An X account is required to sign in to the Developer Console.
Pay-per-use access requires purchasing credits before billed requests succeed.

external_gates.description: Developer Agreement and Policy acceptance is mandatory.
Upfront credit purchase is required under pay-per-usage pricing.

platform_outcomes.description: The Developer Console generates API keys, secrets, and a Bearer Token when the app is created.
X API v2 returns the user lookup JSON when the Bearer Token request succeeds.

excluded.reason: It is a separate access product with different commercial terms.

excluded.item: Official SDK installation for the first request
excluded.reason: The first-request guide treats cURL as the fastest test path.

excluded.item: Reading documentation as a counted action
excluded.reason: Documentation is evidence, not a product interaction.

sources.title: Getting Access
evidence_supported: New App and credential generation including Bearer Token

sources.title: X API pay-per-usage pricing and credits
evidence_supported: Credits purchased upfront in Developer Console

uncertainties.question: Where does the Developer Console place payment-method entry and credit purchase relative to app creation for a brand-new account?
uncertainties.impact: Screen order can change whether billing is before or after credentials and whether the first request is blocked earlier than Getting Access implies.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated Console walkthrough from a fresh developer signup through first successful credited request.

uncertainties.question: What exact fields and possible review states appear in the developer profile and app use-case forms?
uncertainties.impact: Required field counts and potential manual-review gates remain unverified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Current screenshots or a read-only inventory of profile and New App form controls for a new account.

uncertainties.question: Does creating the underlying X consumer account add required fields before console.x.com sign-in when the developer has no X account yet?
uncertainties.impact: The audit boundary is account creation; if X consumer signup is in-path, those fields must be counted.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Confirm whether the measured starting state may assume an existing X account, or document the consumer signup fields.
